borrowed.note: Used modal focus and labelling discipline for a short, high-consequence elevation surface.
Used explicit show-hide treatment for shell-bounded controls instead of hiding scope in profile chrome.

borrowed.appliedTo: switcher row ordering and current-option treatment
borrowed.note: Borrowed current-option and bounded keyboard expectations, but kept rows as full buttons because each row carries rich explanatory text.

borrowed.appliedTo: summary strip and placeholder collapse rules
borrowed.note: Used to keep the control plane readable when the shell narrows or zoom rises.

borrowed.appliedTo: drawer, modal, and masthead control placement
borrowed.note: Used to keep the active control visible while the shell stays mounted beneath it.

borrowed.appliedTo: chip, drawer row, and modal action sizing
borrowed.note: Used to keep the acting-context plane touch-safe and keyboard-safe.
Borrowed restrained high-signal wording for unusual or risky actions without turning the shell into a warning dashboard.
Used as support for preserving stateful control-plane context across navigations instead of rebuilding from scratch.
Used only as secondary support for persistent layout and state-preserving shell thinking.
Used stable selectors and route-level assertions instead of visual timing guesses.
Used to keep the control plane’s semantic structure reviewable.

borrowed.appliedTo: future-proof visual evidence for the control plane
borrowed.note: Used as secondary support for stable evidence capture.
Used separate contexts for reduced-motion and scope-switch scenarios.
Borrowed the emphasis on hierarchy stress tests and restrained chrome, not Linear’s visual styling.
Used only as secondary support for scan-first hierarchy in dense operational surfaces.

rejected.pattern: consumer account dropdown as the only scope control
rejected.reason: Violates the shell law that active scope must stay visible in operational chrome.

rejected.pattern: separate permissions page or settings workflow
rejected.reason: Breaks same-shell continuity and hides freeze or denial inside navigation detours.
Creates alert fatigue and makes ordinary cross-org scope look like an incident.
